name: Momento Cache and Topics
what_it_does: Serverless cache and pub/sub platform requiring zero infrastructure setup, offering instant cache and real-time messaging APIs with per-use pricing.
use_cases: Cache embeddings or retrieved document chunks in agent RAG pipelines to avoid redundant vector DB queries
Broadcast real-time agent status updates to subscribed frontend clients using Momento Topics pub/sub
Store short-lived agent context windows between invocations without provisioning Redis or Memcached clusters
Coordinate multi-agent workflows by publishing task completion events to shared topics that other agents subscribe to
Implement shared rate-limit counters across distributed agent instances using atomic cache increment operations
not_for: Primary persistent database — Momento is a cache with TTL-based eviction, not a durable store
Workloads needing complex query patterns — it is a key-value and pub/sub store with no query language
Applications requiring a self-hosted or VPC-isolated cache for strict network security compliance
best_when: You want a fully managed, zero-configuration cache or pub/sub layer that scales instantly without any infrastructure provisioning or maintenance overhead.
avoid_when: Your caching needs exceed the free tier and cost efficiency is critical at high scale — dedicated Redis clusters may be more economical at sustained high throughput.

auth.notes: Fine-grained auth tokens can be scoped to specific caches, topics, and operations (read/write/publish/subscribe). Token vending machine pattern supported for generating short-lived tokens per agent session.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 50 GB transfer/month, 5 GB storage, unlimited caches and topics
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go at $0.50/GB transfer; no minimum spend requirement
pricing.notes: No idle cost — pricing is based on data transfer, not allocated capacity. Topics pub/sub is included in the same pricing model.

performance.rate_limits: No hard rate limits documented for standard usage; bursting is supported; enterprise limits available on request

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK surfaces typed error classes distinguishing cache misses, auth failures, and server errors. HTTP API returns standard status codes. Error messages are descriptive and actionable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SET operations are idempotent by nature; publish-to-topic has at-most-once semantics. Agents should not assume message delivery guarantees for Topics.
known_agent_gotchas: Topics use at-most-once delivery — messages published while no subscriber is connected are dropped; agents must handle missed messages gracefully
Cache items require an explicit TTL on write — there is no default infinite TTL, which differs from standard Redis behavior and can cause unexpected misses
The REST API is simpler but lacks some SDK features like sorted sets; agents using REST should verify feature parity for their use case
Token vending machine for short-lived scoped tokens requires a backend service — agents cannot safely self-vend tokens without a trusted intermediary
Momento does not support complex data types like Redis sorted sets or streams natively — agents needing these must encode data manually in cache values

security_score_components.security_notes: Fine-grained token scoping per cache/topic/operation is a standout security feature. Short-lived tokens via token vending machine reduce credential exposure. SOC2 and HIPAA compliant.
